title: Compute Perimeter of a Rectangle
description: Find the perimeter of a rectangle by adding all sides or using 2 × (length + width).
condition: A rectangle with known side lengths.
response: Calculate $2l + 2w$ or add all four sides.
label: Principle
example: If $l=10$ and $w=6$, then perimeter $=2(10+6)=32$.

title: Construct Rectangle with Given Area or Perimeter Constraint
description: Create a rectangle that satisfies a stated constraint (same area or same perimeter) while differing in the other measure.
condition: Prompt to draw a rectangle with the same area or perimeter as a given rectangle but a different corresponding measure.
response: Select new side lengths that satisfy the stated constraint and draw the rectangle.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If Rectangle N has area $25$, choose side lengths $1$ and $25$ to keep area $1 \times 25 = 25$ but change the perimeter.

title: Count Boundary Units for Perimeter
description: Determine perimeter by counting unit-length segments around the boundary on a grid.
condition: Given a polygon drawn on a unit grid.
response: Count all outer boundary unit segments.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the boundary has 28 unit segments, the perimeter is 28 units.

title: Count Unit Squares for Area
description: Determine area by counting the number of unit squares covering a shape on a grid.
condition: Given a rectangle drawn on a unit grid.
response: Count total enclosed unit squares.
label: Production / Skill
example: If a rectangle covers 25 grid squares, its area is 25 square units.

title: Define Quadrilateral
description: Recognize that a quadrilateral is a polygon with exactly four sides.
condition: A polygon is shown or described and the task is to determine whether it is a quadrilateral.
response: State that it is a quadrilateral if it has exactly four sides.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 4 sides (e.g., a trapezoid) is a quadrilateral because it has exactly 4 sides.

title: Define Rectangle by Four Right Angles
description: Recognize that a rectangle must have four right angles.
condition: A quadrilateral with four right angles.
response: Classify the shape as a rectangle.
label: Concept
example: If all angles are $90^\circ$, then the quadrilateral is a rectangle.

title: Define Rhombus by Equal Sides
description: Recognize that a rhombus has four equal side lengths.
condition: A quadrilateral with all sides equal in length.
response: Classify the shape as a rhombus.
label: Concept
example: If all 4 sides measure $5$ cm, the quadrilateral is a rhombus.

title: Define Square by Equal Sides and Right Angles
description: Recognize that a square has four equal sides and four right angles.
condition: A quadrilateral with four equal sides and four right angles.
response: Classify the shape as a square.
label: Concept
example: If a quadrilateral has side lengths $8,8,8,8$ and all angles $90^\circ$, it is a square.

title: Divide Remaining Perimeter by 2 for Equal Sides
description: When two equal side lengths together are known as a total, divide by 2 to find the length of one side.
condition: A total length represents the sum of two equal sides (e.g., remaining perimeter after subtracting known sides).
response: Divide the total by 2 to find the length of one side.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the two unknown sides total $28$ feet, compute $28 \div 2 = 14$ feet for each side.

title: Each Grid Segment Represents One Unit
description: On a unit grid, each segment between adjacent grid points represents one unit of length.
condition: A shape is drawn on a unit dot or square grid.
response: Count each segment between adjacent grid points as one unit.
label: Association
example: A side spanning 4 grid segments measures $4$ units.

title: Evaluate Must-Be-True Conditional Statement
description: Determine whether a conditional statement about a shape must always be true based on its defining properties.
condition: A statement of the form "If a quadrilateral is X, then Y" with given side information.
response: Decide whether Y necessarily follows from the definition of X and the given information.
label: Principle
example: If a quadrilateral is a square with side $8$, then perimeter $=4\times8=32$, so the statement is true.

title: Find Factor Pair for Given Area
description: Determine two side lengths whose product equals a given rectangular area.
condition: Given a rectangular area with unknown side lengths.
response: Identify two numbers whose product equals the given area.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If the area is $21$ square yards, a possible pair is $3$ and $7$ because $3 \times 7 = 21$.
notes: Relies on knowledge of multiplication facts.

title: Find Missing Side from Area
description: Determine an unknown side length of a rectangle by dividing the area by the known side length.
condition: Given rectangle area A and one side length s.
response: Compute missing side as A ÷ s.
label: Principle / Model
example: If area is $99$ and one side is $11$, then missing side is $99 \div 11 = 9$.

title: Find Side Pairs from Perimeter
description: Determine possible rectangle side lengths given a fixed perimeter.
condition: A fixed perimeter for a rectangle (e.g., 18 meters of fencing).
response: Find length–width pairs such that $2l + 2w$ equals the given perimeter.
label: Principle
example: If perimeter is $18$, then $2l+2w=18$. One pair is $l=5$, $w=4$ since $2(5)+2(4)=18$.

title: Find Unknown Side from Perimeter
description: Determine a missing side length of a rectangle using the perimeter and a known side.
condition: Given the perimeter of a rectangle and one side length.
response: Substitute into $P = 2(l + w)$ and solve for the unknown side.
label: Principle / Model
example: Given $P = 34$ cm and width $= 6$ cm: $34 = 2(l + 6)$, so $17 = l + 6$, hence $l = 11$ cm.
notes: Requires inverse operations and understanding of perimeter structure.

title: Identify a Quadrilateral by Four Sides
description: Recognize that a polygon with four sides is a quadrilateral.
condition: A polygon with exactly four sides.
response: Classify the shape as a quadrilateral.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 4 sides → quadrilateral.

title: Identify Non-Rectangle by Angle Property
description: Determine that a quadrilateral is not a rectangle if it does not have four right angles.
condition: A quadrilateral is shown and at least one angle is not a right angle.
response: Conclude and state that the shape is not a rectangle because it lacks four right angles.
label: Principle / Model
example: If one angle measures $60^\circ$, then the quadrilateral is not a rectangle because rectangles require four $90^\circ$ angles.
notes: Uses defining attribute to justify exclusion.

title: Multiply Side Length by Number of Equal Sides
description: When all sides of a polygon are equal, multiply one side length by the total number of sides to find the perimeter.
condition: A polygon has equal side lengths and one side length is given.
response: Multiply the side length by the number of sides.
label: Principle / Model
example: A hexagon with side length $8$ inches has perimeter $6 \times 8 = 48$ inches.

title: Opposite Sides of Rectangle Are Equal
description: Use the property that opposite sides of a rectangle have equal lengths.
condition: A rectangle with one pair of opposite sides labeled.
response: Assign the same length to the other pair of opposite sides.
label: Rule
example: If one side is $10$ inches, the opposite side is also $10$ inches.

title: Perimeter as Sum of Side Lengths
description: Perimeter is found by adding the lengths of all sides of a two-dimensional shape.
condition: A closed two-dimensional figure with known side lengths.
response: Add all side lengths to obtain the perimeter.
label: Principle / Model
example: For side lengths $3, 4, 5,$ and $6$, compute $3 + 4 + 5 + 6 = 18$ units.

title: Select All Figures with Given Attribute
description: Evaluate multiple figures and select all that satisfy a stated defining property.
condition: Several shapes are shown and the task is to select all shapes with a specified defining attribute (e.g., all sides equal).
response: Identify each figure that satisfies the defining attribute and select all such figures.
label: Production / Skill
example: Given shapes A–D, select B and C because each has four equal sides.
notes: Requires checking each option against the definition.

title: Subtract Known Side Totals from Perimeter
description: To find unknown side totals, subtract the sum of known sides from the total perimeter.
condition: Total perimeter and some side lengths are known.
response: Add known sides and subtract their sum from the total perimeter.
label: Principle / Model
example: If the perimeter is $54$ feet and two sides total $26$ feet, compute $54 - 26 = 28$ feet remaining.
